title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot discusses a theory with Michael Gaffield regarding a forged will belonging to the deceased Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith. Poirot suggests that Leslie Ferrell, a man with a prior forgery charge, may have forged the will to benefit a close female friend who stands to inherit a fortune. Poirot suspects Ferrell used his relationship with the girl to facilitate the crime, potentially using funds from her employer, Olga Seminova, to fund the scheme. Gaffield, preferring to maintain a peaceful atmosphere in his woods, urges Poirot not to investigate or expose the truth.

Later, Poirot visits Elspeth McKay, who informs him that her brother has been summoned to the police station by Tim Largheon. Elsa Petheridge provides Poirot with a letter from a foreign office regarding Olga Seminova. The letter reveals that Olga’s family is dead and her last correspondence, sent eighteen months prior, mentioned a young man she hoped to marry. Poirot concludes this supports his theory that Leslie Ferrell may have manipulated the situation.

Upon returning to his hotel, Poirot finds Roya DeRoc waiting in the lounge. She is visibly anxious and distraught. She reveals that a child has been killed: Leopold Reynolds. Roya explains that his body was found on a country lane; it appears someone pushed him into a nearby stream and held his head underwater. Poirot draws a connection between this death and a previous incident involving someone named Joyce.
